notes: Aberdeen believes APET should be the first solution acquisition and deployment on way to fully reengineered operation
MRP never architected for demand forecasting or capacity-based production planning; focused on materials flow
Consumer products company: 2.5 weeks to initial pilot; after 3 months shipping costs of $1.2M+ reduced by 17%
Discrete manufacturer in packaging: productivity improvement >150% in 8 weeks; savings >$900,000 expected in year 1
Enterprise with vast internal distribution: realized >$5.5M cost savings from optimized distribution routing
Lighting manufacturer: order costs fell 70% in first 6 months; 12% gross margin improvement to bottom line
American Software 1995 total revenues $79.5M with APET approximately $20M; 300+ installed sites
FACT Inc. 1995 estimated revenues $10M; 180 installed sites
i2 Technologies 1995 estimated revenues $27M; 300+ installed sites
Manugistics 1995 reported total revenues $50M; estimated 60% APET; 300+ installed sites; NASDAQ: MANU
Numetrix 1995 estimated revenues $30M; 285 installed sites
Red Pepper Software 1995 estimated revenues $5M; 50 installed sites; product shipments began 1995
Red Pepper's agent-based optimization technology and partnerships with PeopleSoft, Oracle, SAP suggest strong acquisition potential
Red Pepper Software acquired by PeopleSoft for $225M stock deal in September 1996 — confirmed
i2 Technologies IPO in April 1996 at ~$700M market valuation — confirmed
i2 Technologies acquired by JDA Software for $604M in January 2010 — confirmed
Manugistics acquired by JDA Software in July 2006 — confirmed
Aberdeen believes APET technologies are reliable today and will interoperate with client-server systems of tomorrow
Supply chain planning became standard enterprise software category by 2000; SAP APO, Oracle SCM, i2 widely deployed
Minimum client PC: 486-66 with 16+MB RAM and 1GB Disk; workstation: 32+MB RAM, 2GB Disk
Minimum server: Pentium 32+MB RAM 2GB Disk; Unix server: 64-128MB RAM 4GB Disk
Key success factor: synchronized production flow sharing planning information across organization
Key success factor: build to order, not build to stock; finished goods inventories approach zero
Key success factor: reduced work in process inventories; fewer partial builds
American Software founded 1970, Atlanta GA; publicly traded NASDAQ: AMSWA; 25+ years statistical forecasting tools
i2 partnerships: SAP, Baan, Oracle Manufacturing, Andersen Consulting, Price Waterhouse, CSC, Deloitte & Touche
Red Pepper partnerships: PeopleSoft, Oracle, SSA, SAP, Computer Associates, American Software, Andersen Consulting, Price Waterhouse, CSC
APET integrates with legacy systems like a 'heart bypass' — extracts data, optimizes, feeds results back; minimal disruption
In 1970s, manufacturers in headlong rush to implement MRP and Inventory Control Systems (ICS)
Aberdeen identifies seven key suppliers competing for mind- and market-share in APET market: American Software, FACT, i2, Manugistics, Numetrix, Red Pepper (and others)
Many APET products designed and implemented with latest object-oriented technologies, improving usability and flexibility
APET cost savings can provide much of the funding needed for Enterprise Reengineering efforts
